meta.goal: Sign up for Tripfolk and start planning a first trip

score_rationales.usability: Band 50-65: three high-severity failures on the critical path — renamed CTA, unconfirmed signup, dead-end ending — erode confidence at every step.
score_rationales.cognitive_load: Band 50-65: the commitment step asks users to choose among four options that the UI gives no basis to distinguish.
score_rationales.visual_layout: Band 80-89: every screen individually is clean, grouped correctly, and passes the squint test.
score_rationales.accessibility: Band 80-89: inputs labeled, measured contrast passes (CTA 5.17:1, helper 5.47:1); focus order and keyboard flow unverifiable statically.
score_rationales.content: Band 66-79: copy is plain and helpful per screen, but the flow's key verb changes name mid-journey.
score_rationales.trust_persuasion: Band 66-79: no dark patterns, but a paid commitment is requested with no reassurance about changing or cancelling the plan later.

one_big_thing: No step in this flow ever acknowledges that the previous step worked: the CTA rename, the unmarked plan selection, and the silent landing on an empty dashboard are three symptoms of the same missing-acknowledgement spine.

findings.issue: Four equally weighted plans offer no default or recommendation at the commitment point.
findings.why_it_matters: Choice paralysis lands exactly where hesitation is most expensive, inviting abandonment.
findings.recommendation: Mark one plan as recommended and preselect it.

findings.issue: No plan card shows a selectable or selected state, yet 'Register now' proceeds.
findings.why_it_matters: Users can't tell which plan they're committing to, or whether clicking a card did anything.
findings.recommendation: Make cards visibly selectable and disable Register until one is chosen.

findings.element: feature line on each plan card
findings.issue: All four plans list the identical feature line, differing only in price.
findings.why_it_matters: Nothing explains what paying more buys, so the price ladder reads as arbitrary.
findings.recommendation: State the differentiating limit or feature on each card.

findings.issue: The dashboard greets new users with a large empty box and no next action.
findings.why_it_matters: The activation moment dead-ends; users who just committed have nothing to do.
findings.recommendation: Add a first-run state with a 'Plan your first trip' action.

findings.issue: The flow ends with no confirmation that the account or plan was created.
findings.why_it_matters: Users can't tell whether signup succeeded, which drives duplicate signups and support contacts.
findings.recommendation: Acknowledge the created account and chosen plan before or on the dashboard.

findings.issue: The flow's primary action renames itself from 'Create account' to 'Register now' mid-journey.
findings.why_it_matters: Users can't tell whether step 1 already created the account or 'Register' is something new, so they doubt the whole flow.
findings.recommendation: Use one verb for one commitment across all steps.

findings.issue: The multi-step signup shows no progress indication anywhere.
findings.why_it_matters: Users can't see how much commitment remains, which inflates perceived effort.
findings.recommendation: Add a step indicator to the form and plan screens.

transitions.note: Landing CTA leads to a matching form; label and intent stay consistent.
Primary CTA renames to 'Register now' and nothing acknowledges whether the account from step 1 exists yet.
No confirmation of account or plan; the flow lands silently on an empty dashboard.

missing_states: Selected state on plan cards before 'Register now'
First-run empty state with a primary action on the dashboard
Form validation/error states (absent from the provided sequence)
flow.peak_end: The journey ends at its weakest moment: an empty, unexplained dashboard. The likely emotional peak — committing to a paid plan — is also its most ambiguous step.

clean_checks: WCAG-3.3.2 — email and password fields have persistent visible labels
WCAG-1.4.3 — form CTA measured 5.17:1, helper text 5.47:1 (both pass)
WCAG-2.5.8 — all primary buttons ~50px tall, well above minimum
CNT-10 — password field carries inline guidance ('At least 12 characters.')
NLS-02 — plan names and copy use plain language throughout
NLS-08 — each screen holds a single focused task with no competing content
GST-01/GST-07 — labels, fields, and helper text grouped to match semantics on every screen
GP-06 — consistent header and page structure across all four screens
JAKOB-01 — landing/form/plan layout matches familiar signup conventions
MILL-01 — per-screen information volume is small and chunked
DARK-01..06 — no fake urgency, pre-checked options, confirmshaming, or hidden fees
SHN-02 — generous type sizes and targets throughout
Not statically auditable — form validation behavior, focus order, latency: verify live
